 {"id":"bd-1u1s","title":"Unit Tests: bubbletea core (Cmd, Message, Model)","description":"Add comprehensive unit tests for bubbletea core functionality.

## Current State
bubbletea has basic tests but gaps in:
- Cmd::batch and Cmd::sequence edge cases
- Message type coercion and downcasting
- Model trait default implementations

## Test Requirements

### Cmd Module
- [ ] Cmd::new() with various closure types
- [ ] Cmd::batch() with empty, single, multiple commands
- [ ] Cmd::sequence() ordering guarantees
- [ ] Cmd::blocking() thread behavior
- [ ] Cmd cancellation (if supported)

### Message Module
- [ ] Message::new() with all message types
- [ ] Message::is::<T>() type checking
- [ ] Message::downcast::<T>() success/failure
- [ ] Message cloning and equality

### Key/Mouse Events
- [ ] KeyEvent parsing from raw bytes
- [ ] MouseEvent coordinate handling
- [ ] Modifier key combinations
- [ ] Special keys (F1-F12, arrows, etc.)

## Success Criteria
- 100% coverage of public Cmd API
- 100% coverage of Message API
- All edge cases documented with tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyLantern","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:21.686576405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:25:54.673408772Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:25:54.673341717Z","close_reason":"Added 24 comprehensive unit tests for bubbletea core: Cmd (batch/sequence edge cases, new_optional, blocking, captured values), Message (downcast_ref, Debug format, all built-in types, equality/clone/copy traits). Tests verify 1-command optimization in batch/sequence.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1u1s","depends_on_id":"bd-28co","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:21.715496013Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1wtl","title":"Files: preview pane (viewport)","description":"Add a preview pane for the selected file using `bubbles::viewport`.

Why:
- Makes the Files page feel like a real tool (inspect configs/logs/docs quickly).
- Exercises viewport scrolling + resize behavior.

Preview behavior:
- For plain text:
  - show a bounded preview (first N lines/bytes) with a "truncated" indicator
  - choose a clear wrapping strategy (wrap-by-default is fine; optionally support horizontal scroll)
- Show lightweight metadata (optional but nice):
  - file size, last modified, detected type (text/markdown/binary)
- Resize handling:
  - preview reflows correctly on window resize

Acceptance:
- Large files do not lock up the UI; preview work is bounded.
- Scrolling the preview is smooth and does not interfere with file picker navigation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:19.317694097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:11:21.579944103Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T05:11:21.579876477Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","files","viewport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1wtl","depends_on_id":"bd-201t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:19.337282014Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1wtl","depends_on_id":"bd-fdf3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:14.307425706Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1x3q","title":"Jobs: start/cancel/retry actions via Cmd/AsyncCmd","description":"Make jobs interactive.

Implement actions:
- Start a new job (creates a job + progress updates)
- Cancel job
- Retry failed job

Implementation notes:
- Use the shared domain action API (`demo_showcase data: action API …`) so UI code stays thin.
- bubbletea `Cmd` for sync simulation.
- bubbletea `AsyncCmd` when async feature enabled.

Acceptance:
- Actions produce toasts and update other pages (dashboard/logs).
- Action layer is unit tested independently of the UI.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:20.940826293Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:24:13.108786778Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:24:13.108709945Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase","jobs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1x3q","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:20.955289813Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1x3q","depends_on_id":"bd-3pm6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:38.522320902Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1x3q","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:46.346531972Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1x3q","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:49.013962965Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1xvj","title":"demo_showcase core: Notes scratchpad modal (bubbles::TextArea)","description":"Exercise `bubbles::TextArea` in a way that feels like a real product feature.

Feature idea:
- A modal "Notes" scratchpad to write an incident note / deploy note.
- Supports multi-line editing, copy, clear, save.
- Saving the note emits a structured log entry and a toast.

Why this belongs in core:
- Provides a compelling demo of text editing beyond single-line inputs.
- Gives users a practical pattern they can copy into real apps.

Acceptance:
- Accessible via a global key (defined in UX keymap).
- Uses `bubbles::TextArea` (not huh) so we prove both stacks.
- Saving integrates with the log stream + notifications.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:29.295951524Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:40:54.896615005Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","demo_showcase","textarea","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:46.342854462Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-28jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:43.822250299Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:29.315341950Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-3pm6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:45.081658950Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:42.571125789Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:41.284606640Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1ynf","title":"Wizard: design the multi-step workflow","description":"Design the wizard flow so it demonstrates real value.

Suggested narrative: "Deploy a service" or "Create a new job".

Steps (example):
1) pick service + environment (select)
2) configure params (inputs + textarea)
3) confirm + risk acknowledgement (confirm)
4) run (shows progress/spinner) + completion summary

Acceptance:
- Each step maps to a huh component and produces meaningful state changes in the app.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:00.625870909Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:15.113174531Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:15.113086597Z","close_reason":"Design spec created in docs/demo_showcase/WIZARD_DESIGN.md - 6-step workflow mapping all huh components, committed as e6c0667","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","forms","huh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ynf","depends_on_id":"bd-2omz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:00.655155554Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ynf","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:39.159222728Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ywx","title":"glamour: Validate language aliases on registration","description":"# Task: Validate Language Aliases on Registration

## Problem Statement
The language_alias() builder method accepts any strings without validation:

```rust
// Lines 623-630
pub fn language_alias(mut self, alias: impl Into<String>, language: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    self.syntax_config
        .language_aliases
        .insert(alias.into(), language.into());
    self
}
```

No checks for:
1. Whether target language is actually supported
2. Whether alias forms a cycle (A -> B -> A)
3. Whether alias shadows a built-in language name

## Impact Analysis
- **Silent failures**: Alias to non-existent language just doesn't highlight
- **Confusing behavior**: Cycles could cause infinite loops or unexpected results
- **Debugging difficulty**: No error message explains why highlighting isn't working

## Solution Design

### Validation on Registration
```rust
pub fn language_alias(
    mut self,
    alias: impl Into<String>,
    language: impl Into<String>,
) -> Result<Self, GlamourError> {
    let alias = alias.into();
    let language = language.into();
    
    // Check target language exists
    if !self.syntax_set_contains(&language) {
        return Err(GlamourError::InvalidLanguage(language));
    }
    
    // Check for cycles
    if self.would_create_cycle(&alias, &language) {
        return Err(GlamourError::AliasCycle { alias, language });
    }
    
    self.syntax_config.language_aliases.insert(alias, language);
    Ok(self)
}

fn would_create_cycle(&self, alias: &str, target: &str) -> bool {
    let mut current = target;
    let mut visited = HashSet::new();
    visited.insert(alias);
    
    while let Some(next) = self.syntax_config.language_aliases.get(current) {
        if !visited.insert(next.as_str()) {
            return true;  // Cycle detected
        }
        current = next;
    }
    false
}
```

### Backward Compatibility Option
Keep existing method but add validated version:
```rust
// Existing (for compatibility)
pub fn language_alias(self, alias: impl Into<String>, language: impl Into<String>) -> Self

// New validated version
pub fn try_language_alias(
    self,
    alias: impl Into<String>,
    language: impl Into<String>,
) -> Result<Self, GlamourError>
```

## Implementation Steps
1. Add validation logic to language_alias()
2. Add GlamourError variants for invalid language and cycles
3. Consider deprecating unchecked version
4. Update documentation with valid language list
5. Add tests for validation

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn alias_to_invalid_language_fails() {
    let result = GlamourRenderer::new()
        .try_language_alias("rs", "nonexistent");
    assert!(matches!(result, Err(GlamourError::InvalidLanguage(_))));
}

#[test]
fn alias_cycle_detected() {
    let result = GlamourRenderer::new()
        .try_language_alias("a", "b")?
        .try_language_alias("b", "c")?
        .try_language_alias("c", "a");  // Creates cycle
    assert!(matches!(result, Err(GlamourError::AliasCycle { .. })));
}

#[test]
fn valid_alias_succeeds() {
    let result = GlamourRenderer::new()
        .try_language_alias("rs", "rust");
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Invalid target language detected with clear error
- [ ] Alias cycles detected and rejected
- [ ] Backward-compatible API (old method still works)
- [ ] Documentation lists valid language names
- [ ] Tests cover validation scenarios","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:21:13.883578694Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:05:45.211031393Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:05:45.210961983Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["configuration","glamour","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ywx","depends_on_id":"bd-8wlh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:21:13.902020466Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
